records_en_draft.summary_en_draft: The 140-item extension adds more concrete task, environment, and role-responsibility context for Realistic interests. It does not override the 60-item result, compare raw-score deltas, or strengthen the conclusion.
records_en_draft.task_activity_card_en_draft: Task actions: notice whether you want to keep engaging with building, testing, maintaining, troubleshooting, prototyping, or working with visible real-world constraints.
records_en_draft.environment_card_en_draft: Environment signals: observe pace, collaboration density, structure, feedback, and how real objects, tools, sites, materials, and visible outcomes show up in the work setting.
records_en_draft.role_responsibility_card_en_draft: Role responsibility: notice whether you prefer executing, explaining, expressing, supporting, moving things forward, or organizing reliable steps.
records_en_draft.example_question_en_draft: If you continue observing Realistic, would you rather look at task actions, environment conditions, or role responsibilities?
records_en_draft.cannot_claim_en_draft: Do not frame 140Q as more accurate, as overturning 60Q, or as evidence of ability, job fit, organizational fit, or career outcome.

task_examples_en_draft: Choose one small action related to building, testing, maintaining, troubleshooting, prototyping, or working with visible real-world constraints and try it for 15 minutes.
Notice whether the real object, context, material, audience, or visible output makes you want to continue.
Record energy, drain, curiosity, and avoidance without judging performance.
records_en_draft.low_risk_validation_en_draft: Use a small version to observe whether you want more contact with this activity pattern. This observes interest only; it does not prove ability or create a career conclusion.

duration_options_en_draft.15min: Observe one small action and record only the change in energy.
duration_options_en_draft.30min: Finish a minimal version and write what you wanted to continue, stop, or move to a different environment.
duration_options_en_draft.2h: Do a light interview or observation in a real setting and record task, environment, and role-responsibility signals.
duration_options_en_draft.1week: Repeat a similar activity twice in one week and compare interest stability rather than performance quality.

records_en_draft.overlap_reading_en_draft: 产品 / 用户研究｜想了解 is a direction the user wants to move toward, not a conclusion assigned by the system. It can be read alongside current interest signals, but it must not rewrite the measured Holland Code or scores.
reality_questions_en_draft: Which part of this aspiration attracts you most: the task action, environment, people served, or role responsibility?
If you could run only one 30-minute experiment, which part would you test first?
What does the current result support observing next, and what should you avoid over-reading for now?

dimensions_en_draft.core_drive_en_draft: Realistic interest is read as an interest signal around real objects, tools, sites, materials, and visible outcomes. It is not an ability score or a fixed identity. The useful question is where this kind of evidence gives you energy, curiosity, or a reason to keep observing.
dimensions_en_draft.positive_value_en_draft: When used well, this signal can help turn vague preferences into observable work-style evidence around building, testing, maintaining, troubleshooting, prototyping, or working with visible real-world constraints.
dimensions_en_draft.real_world_cost_en_draft: The cost appears when the environment forces this signal into a narrow stereotype or removes the conditions that made the interest meaningful.
dimensions_en_draft.high_score_reading_en_draft: If Realistic is high, you may be more easily pulled toward building, testing, maintaining, troubleshooting, prototyping, or working with visible real-world constraints. Read this as an invitation to observe interest, not as proof of skill or job fit.
dimensions_en_draft.medium_score_reading_en_draft: If Realistic sits in the middle, it may work as a supporting context rather than the main entry point. Use a small task to see whether the interest holds.
dimensions_en_draft.low_score_safe_reading_en_draft: Low Realistic does not mean you cannot learn or succeed in related tasks. It only means this may not be your most natural current interest entry point.
work_activity_examples_en_draft: Try one small task involving building, testing, maintaining, troubleshooting, prototyping, or working with visible real-world constraints.
Observe what gives energy, what drains energy, and what you want to keep exploring.
Compare the task action, environment, and role responsibility separately.
possible_drains_en_draft: being pushed toward a career conclusion
having no room to test interest safely
dimensions_en_draft.common_misread_en_draft: The common misread is treating an interest signal as ability, identity, or destiny. RIASEC does not measure ability and must not be used for hiring or screening.
dimensions_en_draft.action_advice_en_draft: Choose one small low-risk experiment and record energy, drain, curiosity, and avoidance. Do not turn a single result into a career decision.

dimensions_en_draft.real_world_cost_en_draft: The cost appears when the environment forces this signal into a narrow stereotype or removes the conditions that made the interest meaningful.
